description:
Fix Polish Character Encoding Issues on Webpage

Resolve character encoding problems causing Polish special characters to display incorrectly or not appear at all throughout the webpage.

Requirements:
- Identify all locations where Polish characters (ą, ć, ę, ł, ń, ó, ś, ź, ż and their uppercase variants) are missing or displaying as garbled text
- Verify HTML document is declared with UTF-8 encoding in the meta charset tag
- Ensure server is sending correct Content-Type header with UTF-8 charset
- Check that source files are saved in UTF-8 encoding format
- Verify database connection and queries use UTF-8 character set if content is dynamically loaded
- Replace any incorrectly encoded text with properly encoded Polish characters
- Test display across different browsers to confirm characters render correctly
- Validate that user input forms properly handle and store Polish characters

Success Criteria:
- All Polish special characters display correctly throughout the webpage
- No placeholder characters (�, ?, boxes) appear where Polish letters should be
- Text is readable and maintains proper Polish spelling and grammar
